title: Recursive Containers, Stack Parsing, Depth, and Recovery · Stakeholder Software Mission
summary: A multilingual community documentation editor. They need an import-safe atomic command that admits authorized UTF-8 content, validates metadata, builds a source-mapped bounded document tree, parses a declared Markdown subset, reports malformed nesting precisely, and preserves literal author evidence without pretending full CommonMark conformance. Planned artifact: Deliver Source-Mapped Content Pipeline and Parser Foundation with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-con…
objectives: Define reader, task, content owner, publication decision, consequence of error, and observable success before selecting syntax or tooling.
Build into a fresh staging root, verify it, replace output deliberately, and preserve the prior good artifact on failure.
Validate allowed keys, required values, types, formats, unknown fields, duplicates, defaults, and source locations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Error Recovery
steps.instruction:

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-error-recovery-16fn42
# Competency: ssg-error-recovery.
# Case 7752: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_error_recovery_16fn42(containers_opened: int = 7, containers_closed: int = 7, max_depth: int = 6, recovery_points: int = 2, stalled_positions: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_16fn42 = "16fn42-24nf61-7752"
    assert evidence_variant_16fn42
    evidence_axes_16fn42 = (
        "reader16fn42",
        "source7752",
        "parse24nf61",
        "route16fn427752",
        "artifact775216fn42",
        "recovery24nf617752",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_16fn42)) == 6
    if containers_opened < 0 or containers_closed < 0:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-count"
    if containers_closed != containers_opened:
        return False, containers_closed, "container-balance"
    if max_depth < 0 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    if recovery_points < 1 or stalled_positions > 0:
        return False, stalled_positions, "recovery-progress"
    assert containers_closed == containers_opened
    return True, max_depth, "recoverable-nesting"
